title: 💻 Claude Code Security Best Practices - Backslash
content:

# Claude Code Security Best Practices. Claude Code is a popular agentic coding assistant designed by Anthropic to run in a developer’s terminal, offering deep codebase analysis, code generation, project planning, and automation through natural language prompts. ## **Why Claude Code Security Matters**. When AI becomes part of your coding environment, the IDE is no longer “just a text editor.” It’s an insider with:. * **The ability to run commands**. That’s not just convenience — it’s risk. A single poisoned prompt or misconfigured setting can turn Claude Code from your coding partner into a threat actor. This blog is your guide to staying safe: how to configure Claude Code, what traps to avoid, and how to combine internal and external defenses. Every time it runs a command or reads a file, it’s acting with your permissions. ## **The Heart of Security: `managed-settings.json`**. Like most AI coders, Claude Code has a variety of configuration settings for security. Claude Code’s security settings live here:.

title: 💻 Context is the new skill: lessons from the Claude Code best practices guide | AI @ Sulat.com
content:

# Context is the new skill: lessons from the Claude Code best practices guide. ## *The newly published best practices guide captures everything Anthropic has learned from watching developers work with their AI coding agent*. is the place to be. The newly published *Claude Code Best Practices* guide represents months of observation packed into a single resource. That’s a lot of data and telemetry on what works and what doesn’t. What makes this guide worth reading isn’t the tips themselves. It’s what they reveal about the real constraints of working with AI coding agents. ## The core constraint: context is everything. > *Claude’s context window fills up fast, and performance degrades as it fills.*. They’re about **signal-to-noise ratio** (a term you hear a lot, especially in SysAdmin and DevOps). The guide is clear about this. It loses track of what you actually asked for. Think of context like a whiteboard in a meeting room.

content:

[Skip to main content](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/blog/claude-code-best-practices-lessons-from-real-projects/#main-content). [Audio 1](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/blog/claude-code-best-practices-lessons-from-real-projects/). ![Image 3: Claude Code Best Practices: Lessons From Real Projects](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/images/blog/claude-code-best-practices-lessons-from-real-projects/image-01.webp). ![Image 5: Propel Kanban board showing Backlog, In Progress, Blocked, and Completed columns with task cards](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/images/blog/claude-code-best-practices-lessons-from-real-projects/image-02.webp). When I built it, I used Claude Code alongside [BMAD](https://github.com/bmadcode/BMAD-METHOD), an [AI SDLC framework](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/blog/ai-driven-sdlc/) that guides you through product design, user flows, and specifications before you write a single line of code. ![Image 6: BMAD available commands table showing brainstorming, party mode, help, and review workflows](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/images/blog/claude-code-best-practices-lessons-from-real-projects/image-03.webp). ![Image 7: BMAD brainstorming session complete with 36 ideas, MVP scope, and 4-phase roadmap](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/images/blog/claude-code-best-practices-lessons-from-real-projects/image-04.webp). ![Image 8: mac-folder-sync menu bar app showing sync status, last sync time, and configuration options](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/images/blog/claude-code-best-practices-lessons-from-real-projects/image-05.webp). ![Image 9: A comic page drawn in dog-man style featuring a cat with acid poop superpowers](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/images/blog/claude-code-best-practices-lessons-from-real-projects/image-06.webp). I wrote a full breakdown of my setup, the About Me file, the anti-AI style guide, the CLAUDE.md, and the LinkedIn post drafter skill in [How I Use Claude Cowork to Write With AI in My Voice](https://ranthebuilder.cloud/blog/how-i-use-claude-cowork-to-write-with-ai-in-my-voice/), with all four gists included so you can fork and adapt them for your own voice.

title: 💻 Batch Processing Skill for Claude Code | Scale AI Workflows
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Batch Processing. Optimizes large-scale task execution through agent swarms, API batch processing, and intelligent loops to maximize efficiency and reduce costs. ### Related Skills. Manages Discord operations including messaging, reactions, and channel management directly through Claude. Automates code formatting and linting checks to ensure code quality and CI compliance before committing. Discover MCP servers that connect MCP clients like Claude and Cursor to your favorite tools. Browse the MCP Market to get started. The Batch Processing skill for Claude Code empowers developers to handle high-volume tasks—such as reviewing dozens of files or processing hundreds of documents—without linear increases in time or token costs. It provides a strategic framework for selecting the optimal execution method based on scale: utilizing agent swarms for rapid parallel processing of 5-20 items, the Anthropic API Batch for 50% cost savings on massive datasets, or sequential loops for tasks with complex dependencies. Sync skills to **Claude Cowork**, **Claude Code**, **Codex**, and more.

title: 💻 Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice - SmartScope
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

* /batch Command: Parallel Execution of Large-Scale Code Changes. * Headless Mode (claude -p): CI/CD and Script Integration. # Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice¶. The `/batch` command in Claude Code shines when you need to apply the same transformation across hundreds of files. * The `/batch` command executes codebase-wide migrations safely using **parallel agents + Git Worktree** isolation. * Headless mode (`claude -p`) provides **non-interactive integration** for CI/CD pipelines and shell scripts. Intermediate to advanced Claude Code users looking to scale up with large-scale changes and CI/CD integration. The latter is a non-interactive execution mode for embedding AI processing into CI/CD pipelines. | Aspect | /batch Command | Headless Mode (claude -p) |. Choose headless mode when embedding AI processing into existing CI/CD pipelines. `/batch` is a slash command executed within an interactive Claude Code session1. ## Headless Mode (claude -p): CI/CD and Script Integration¶.

title: 💻 Claude Code /simplify and /batch Commands Guide
content:
Claude Code 技术干货

# Claude Code /simplify and /batch: Bundled Commands That Ship Code Faster. Claude Code /simplify runs 3-agent code review on your changes. The code works, but you know there's duplicated logic, a few functions that could be cleaner, and some inefficient patterns you didn't have time to address. # After implementing a feature # After implementing a feature/simplify/simplify  # Focus on a specific concern # Focus on a specific concern/simplify focus on memory efficiency/simplify  focus  on  memory  efficiency. If you need something bigger -- migrating an entire codebase from one framework to another, or applying a change across dozens of files -- that's what `/batch` is for. ## What Claude Code /simplify Does. Boris Cherny, the Claude Code PM, put it well: these bundled commands "automate much of the work it used to take to shepherd a pull request to production." `/simplify` is the automated code review step between "it works" and "it's ready to merge.".

► Join the HerMpire Builders Community to download the free Content Batch Day System skill + manual + implementation guide 👉 https://skool.com/digitalmpire-builders
► Tap into your 14-Day Free Skool Trial: https://www.skool.com/signup?ref=070272b1669f463f91939c76d77d798e

If you want to batch a week of content in one day with Claude, this video is your step-by-step. No code required, no tech background needed, and your content will actually sound like you because we're training it on your voice, your offers, and your audience from the jump.

I walk you through how to use the Content Batch Day System skill inside both Claude desktop and Claude Code. You can start from a topic idea, drop in a blog post URL, or upload a video transcript and it will generate Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, carousels, and community posts all in one session.

Whether you're using Claude desktop or running Claude Code off your computer, I show you both paths so you can choose what works for your setup. And everything you need to get started is completely free in the HerMpire Builders community.

What's inside this video:

- How to install the Content Batch Day System skill in Claude desktop
- How to set up the skill inside Claude Code for faster, file-based batching
- How to build your content pack (brand voice, avatar, offers) so your AI output actually sounds like you
- How to use a blog post, YouTube transcript, or topic idea as your content source
- What types of content it produces across every platform

You don't need to set this up every time. You build the content pack once, and from that point on Claude knows who you are and what you create. That's how you go from spending hours on content every week to getting it all done in one sitting.

Get the skill, the manual, and the implementation guide for free in Builders. Link above.

## Use saved searches to filter your results more quickly. # claude-batch-toolkit. Send non-urgent work to the Anthropic Batch API (or Vertex AI) at **50% cost** — directly from Claude Code. Works with the Anthropic API directly or through Google Cloud's Vertex AI. This shows what will be removed, asks for confirmation, and preserves your results in `~/.claude/batches/results/`. If you prefer not to run the install script — or need to install in a restricted environment — follow these steps to set up each component by hand. | **curl** | Polls the Anthropic API from statusline | `brew install curl` or `apt-get install curl` |. #### Step 2: Install the MCP server. #### Step 4 *(optional)*: Install the statusline script. Skip this step if you don't want batch job counts in your Claude Code status bar. #### Step 6: Register the MCP server in `~/.claude.json`. #### Step 7 *(optional)*: Configure the statusline in `~/.claude/settings.json`.
